normalized_title: Planning Bulletin 112/2024: Single House Development Standards (R25 to R40) - Development Zones (R-MD Codes)
scope_summary: Sets out the R-MD Codes: acceptable variations to the deemed-to-comply provisions of the Residential Design Codes Volume 1 for single houses on R25 to R40 coded lots in development (greenfield/structure plan) zones, for consistent application across WA. The R-MD Codes vary R-Codes Part B clauses 5.1.2, 5.1.3 and 5.2.1 (building and garage setbacks), 5.1.4 (open space), 5.3.3 (parking), 5.4.1 (visual privacy) and 5.4.2 (solar access), and are implemented by local planning policy (model LPP at Appendix 2, exempt from advertising) or scheme amendment; the former R-MD R60 provisions were removed following the 2024 R-Codes amendments.

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum lot width for double garage
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: front-loaded single house with double garage, sole access from primary street
as viewed from the street, front-loaded lots with frontages 10.5-12 m
R-MD R40 and R30 front load single houses
R-MD R25 front load single houses
walls of height 3.5 m or less (subject to wall length and major openings), R-MD R25-R40
R-MD R40 and R30 single houses

key_numeric_standards.value: 10% of lot size or 20 m2 (whichever greater), minimum 3 m length or width dimension; minimum courtyard 20 m2 (R40), 24 m2 (R30), 30 m2 (R25)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R-MD single houses, directly accessible from a habitable room

key_numeric_standards.value: 0.5 (nil street setback provided laneway is minimum 6 m wide)
4.5 from primary street (reducible to 4 where footpath/shared path is more than 0.5 m from street boundary); 1.5 from secondary street; or 0.5 behind dwelling alignment

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: fences within the primary street setback area, measured from natural ground level on the street side
single houses; no maximum where wall height 3.5 m or less / shadow confined to front half of lot

key_numeric_standards.value: 4.5 to bedrooms and studies; 6 to other major openings; 7.5 to balconies or similar
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: major openings and unenclosed active habitable spaces (R-Codes cl 5.4.1 C1.1 applies with reduced distances of 3/4.5/6 m in some cases)

cross_references.cited: Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015

quality_flags: Appendix 1 standards table extracted with severe multi-column interleaving; values above were reconstructed from legible fragments and clause-to-typology attribution should be checked against the source PDF before high-stakes use
Disclaimer block states 'Version 2 published 10 April 2024' while the title block is dated 8 March 2024 - the April reissue aligns with the R-Codes Vol.1 2024 gazettal
instrument_aliases.json maps 'Liveable Neighbourhoods' to DC-009, but manifest DC-009 is a different instrument - alias not used
